title: Claytonia interrupta
taxonomy: Claytonia interrupta is classified within the family Montiaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the miner's lettuce family. This classification is based on its morphological and molecular characteristics. Claytonia interrupta is further classified as a species within the genus Claytonia, which includes various species of spring-beans and winter purslane. The genus Claytonia is often placed in the order Caryophyllales, which comprises a diverse range of flowering plants. Within the order Caryophyllales, the family Montiaceae is one of several families that exhibit similar characteristics and evolutionary relationships.

intro: Claytonia interrupta, also known as "Desert Spring Beauty" or "Sandwort", is a small, flowering plant that belongs to the purslane family (Portulacaceae). The health benefits of Claytonia interrupta include its potential to support eye health due to its high content of beta-carotene, a precursor to vitamin A, as well as its possible anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
